title: Executive Order 9691—Directing the Civil Service Commission to Resume Operations Under the Civil Service Rules, and Authorizing the Adoption of Special Regulations During the Transitional Period

flags.justification: The Civil Service Commission exercises substantial discretion, but as the standard, well-established merit-system regulator operating under long-standing statutory constraints (Civil Service Act, Veterans' Preference Act) with detailed procedural rules set directly by this order.

Sec. 4 provides a genuine correction mechanism: 'upon establishment before the Commission that this order of selection has not been followed in any case, the person appointed may be removed' — an explicit, textually specific accountability provision.
Multiple related sub-provisions (resuming exams, veterans' preference, temporary-appointment rules, status conversion) all coherently serve one purpose: normalizing civil service operations postwar.
Extensive, precise specificity throughout — exact preference-point thresholds, exact deadlines, exact eligibility conditions.
The detailed preference-ordering rules and Sec. 4's removal-for-noncompliance mechanism are specifically designed to prevent circumvention.

Multiple explicit review mechanisms with concrete deadlines: the Commission must submit recommendations for general Civil Service Rules revision by July 1, 1946 (Sec. 3), recommendations for Schedule A revision by the same date (Sec. 7), and Sec. 7 sets a hard cutoff of December 31, 1946 for excepted-position continuity absent formal inclusion.
Sec. 9 explicitly preserves reemployment rights 'heretofore acquired by any person under any law or Executive order' — no existing right or oversight mechanism is removed.

Sec. 4 explicitly requires departments and agencies to 'keep such records and make such reports as will show that the preferences... have been granted' — a textually explicit compliance-verification mechanism, paired with the removal remedy for violations.
A well-scoped, appropriately bounded transitional order restoring normal peacetime civil service operations; no novel or expansive authority claim.
The Civil Service Commission operates within its own established jurisdiction; no other agency's mandate is redirected.

Sec. 7's exception process for certain professional/scientific/technical positions requires public Federal Register notice and a hard December 31, 1946 cutoff for formal inclusion — narrow, transparent, and bounded, not an undermining carve-out.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/22 = 0.0%. One of the most exemplary designs coded to date — extensive specificity, multiple explicit review deadlines, and a built-in removal-for-noncompliance enforcement mechanism, despite covering a genuinely complex transitional subject matter.
